content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both genuinely need edits in both files (compression touches Cache.__init__/get/put + configure_cache; TTL touches Cache.__init__/memory+disk layers + configure_cache), so true file-level disjointness isn't achievable. Proposing we both keep both files but use non-overlapping code regions: I will ONLY (1) add new trailing kwargs to Cache.__init__: compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy (2) add private _encode_value/_decode_value helpers (3) touch the disk_cache read/write lines inside get()/put() to route through those helpers (4) add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() and the two Cache(...) call sites, appended after existing params. I will NOT touch memory_cache construction, TTLCache logic, or add/modify __all__. Please confirm you'll add your ttl param/set_ttl similarly as additive, separate lines (not modifying my added lines), e.g. your own kwargs in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache appended separately, and TTLCache swap for memory_cache is yours to own. If that's workable, ACCEPT and we'll both proceed independently.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished and tested compression + eviction knobs. Summary: (1) cache.py: added gzip/pickle/zlib imports, module-level magic-header constants (_CACHE_MAGIC=b'DSPC', codec bytes for none/gzip/zlib); Cache.__init__ gained trailing 'compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None' params (validated against {None,'gzip','zlib'}), self.compress attribute, cull_limit/eviction_policy forwarded to FanoutCache only if not None; added set_compression()/_encode_disk_value()/_decode_disk_value() methods; get()/put() now route disk_cache reads/writes through these helpers (memory cache untouched - still stores live objects). (2) __init__.py: configure_cache() gained trailing 'compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy' params forwarded into Cache(...) as kwargs; DSPY_CACHE module-level construction left untouched (defaults unchanged). Verified: gzip/zlib round-trip integrity, legacy header-less entries still load, on-disk size measurably reduced with compression, dynamic set_compression works on old+new entries, pydantic object types preserved, existing test suite (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py) all pass. Did not touch memory_cache/TTLCache/__all__. Ready to merge whenever you are.
